# slicer: Sliced Wasserstein Distances for Regression

`slicer` provides functions for computing sliced Wasserstein distances
as well as one-dimensional Wasserstein distances between empirical
distributions represented as matrices. These distances can be fed to a
Gaussian process regression model, which currently accepts the Gaussian
radial basis function kernel as well as the Matérn kernels with
smoothness parameters $`\nu = 0.5, 1.5, 2.5`$.

## Installation

You can install the development version of slicer from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r

# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("janhove/slicer")
```

## Use

Please refer to the vignette for a brief tutorial.

# Package index

## All functions

- [`compute_all_distances()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/compute_all_distances.md)
  : Pairwise Wasserstein Distances Along Projection Directions Between
  Several Empirical Distributions
- [`fit_gpr()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/fit_gpr.md) :
  Gaussian Process Fitting to Test Data with Tuned Hyperparameters
- [`generate_directions()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/generate_directions.md)
  : Generate Random Projection Directions
- [`gpr_predict()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/gpr_predict.md)
  : Predict Outcomes Using Gaussian Process Regression
- [`kernel_sum()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/kernel_sum.md)
  : Sum of Kernels
- [`matern()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/matern.md) :
  Matérn Kernels
- [`matern_multiple()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/matern_multiple.md)
  : Sum of Matérn Kernels
- [`nlpd_gpr()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/nlpd_gpr.md)
  : Negative Log Predictive Density for Gaussian Process Regression
  Models
- [`nw_corner_distance()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/nw_corner_distance.md)
  : One-Dimensional Wasserstein Distance Using North-West Corner
  Algorithm
- [`rbf()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/rbf.md) :
  Gaussian Radial Basis Function Kernel
- [`rbf_multiple()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/rbf_multiple.md)
  : Sum of Gaussian Radial Basis Function Kernels
- [`sliced_wasserstein()`](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/reference/sliced_wasserstein.md)
  : Sliced Wasserstein Distance Between Two Empirical Distributions

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [(Sliced) Wasserstein - Gaussian process regression
  workflow](https://janhove.github.io/slicer/articles/sliced-wasserstein-workflow.md):
